title: Dortmanna decurrens

It is native to North America, specifically in the western and central regions. The genus Dortmanna is a small group of flowering plants that are closely related to the evening primrose. Dortmanna decurrens is often considered a rare or uncommon species, with limited distribution and habitat. Further classification and research may be necessary to fully understand the botanical relationships of Dortmanna decurrens.
properties: Dortmanna decurrens, also known as the golden-rimmed buttonbush, has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. However, more research is needed to fully explore its therapeutic potential. The plant contains compounds that may have antioxidant and antiseptic effects. Some studies suggest that Dortmanna decurrens extracts may help protect against certain infections and promote wound healing.
